channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Prince Andrew's antics and scandals have tried royal patience, triggering headlines and suspicions
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew was forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya as thousands turn out to mourn former PM Odinga
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu ahead of the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, after five people died in the last two public funeral events.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French police arrest 4 in alleged plot targeting exiled Russian activist and Putin critic
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Police in France detained four people suspected in a plot targeting exiled Russian rights activist Vladimir Osechkin, who exposes abuses in Russian prisons, France's national anti-terror prosecution office said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orbán celebrates Hungary as 'the only place in Europe' where a Trump-Putin meeting can be held
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary's nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday celebrated his country's status as the host of upcoming talks between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russia's Vladimir Putin, a meeting where the two leaders are expected to discuss an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Iran’s execution rate tops 1,000 this year as death row inmates launch hunger strike
item.description: Iranian death row prisoners call on U.N. Secretary General António Guterres to intervene as the regime executes over 1,000 people since the start of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King to pray with pope in first since 1534
item.description: VATICAN CITY/LONDON, Oct 17 (Reuters) - Britain's King Charles and Queen Camilla will meet Pope Leo at the Vatican next week in a visit seen as a sign of closening ties between the Catholic Church and the Anglican Communion, five centuries after their turbulent separation.
item.dc:creator: Joshua McElwee and Muvija M, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Trump explains father's hot mic moment with Indonesian president
item.description: Eric Trump sought to clarify Thursday what President Trump and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto talked about during a hot mic moment when the commander in chief's son came up during a conversation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Mongolia's prime minister ousted after 4 months in ruling party feud
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Mongolia ’s parliament has voted to oust the prime minister in an unusually public power struggle within the ruling Mongolian People’s Party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kenyans bid farewell to statesman and democracy activist Raila Odinga at state funeral
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Presidents and representatives of African heads of state joined thousands of mourners at a state funeral service Friday for Kenya’s former Prime Minister Raila Odinga, the democratic reformer who died this week in India at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian barrage causes blackouts in Ukraine as Zelenskyy seeks Trump's help
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia battered Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in its latest heavy bombardment of the country’s power grid, authorities said Thursday, as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy prepared to ask President Donald Trump at a White House m
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas captors once held ordinary jobs
item.description: Israeli hostage Tal Shoham describes increased faith and gratitude after enduring torture and starvation during 500 days held by Hamas in Gaza tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Teen street musician jailed for leading anti-Putin song performance in St. Petersburg: report
item.description: Diana Loginova faces charges for organizing an unauthorized public gathering and discrediting the Russian military after performing an anti-Putin song.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: 'No choice but to go in and kill' Hamas if Gaza bloodshed doesn't stop
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that if Hamas does not heed repeated warnings to stop bloodshed in Gaza, the U.S. will have “no choice” but to use lethal force. “If Hamas continues to kill people in Gaza, which was not the Deal, we will have no choice but to go in and kill them.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russia targets Ukraine energy facilities ahead of Trump meeting with Zelensky
item.description: Russia hit Ukraine’s energy facilities with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles Thursday, a day ahead of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s meeting with President Trump at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Trump to meet Putin in Hungary in effort to end 'inglorious' war
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he intends to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Hungary in the coming weeks to discuss efforts to end the war in Ukraine. Trump made the announcement following a lengthy phone call with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Afghanistan’s Taliban government blames Pakistan for twin drone strikes on Kabul
item.description: KABUL, Afghanistan (AP) — Afghanistan’s Taliban government said Thursday that Pakistan had carried out two drone strikes on Kabul the previous day, just before the two neighbors announced a ceasefire following days of fighting that killed dozens in both countries and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: SIDDIQULLAH ALIZAI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former security guard at US Embassy overseas is convicted of spying for Russia and Iran
item.description: A former U.S. Embassy security guard in Norway was reportedly sentenced to three years and seven months after allegedly spying for Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of last female Hamas hostage and IDF soldier handed over to Israel
item.description: Israel received two more deceased hostages' bodies, including that of Inbar Hayman, who was considered to be the last female hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US blasts China's expansion of rare earth export controls
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Top U.S. officials on Wednesday blasted China's major expansion of rare earth export controls as a threat to global supply chains, but said Beijing could still change course and avoid steps by Washington to decouple from the world's second-largest economy. U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Lawder and Andrea Shalal, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pakistan and Taliban agree to 48-hour ceasefire after renewed fighting kills dozens
item.description: A temporary ceasefire has been agreed to after border violence escalated between Pakistan and Afghanistan over the weekend as both sides blame each other for attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan announce ceasefire after civilians killed
item.description: ISLAMABAD/KABUL, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a "temporary ceasefire" on Wednesday, Islamabad said, after an airstrike and ground fighting sent tensions between the South Asian neighbors soaring, killing more than a dozen civilians.
item.dc:creator: By Saleem Ahmed, Mushtaq Ali and Mohammad Yunus Yawar, NewsNation

item.title: Remains released by Hamas not a hostage, Israel's military says
item.description: The original framework of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire required Hamas to return all 28 deceased hostages Monday.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Afghanistan says more than a dozen civilians killed in renewed border fighting with Pakistan
item.description: The countries have traded fire along the border since Saturday, when dozens were killed across multiple border regions.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel frees some Gaza medical staff, but a prominent hospital chief remains imprisoned
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Under Gaza’s ceasefire deal, Israel freed dozens of doctors, nurses, paramedics and other medical personnel seized during raids on hospitals. But more than 100 remain in Israeli prisons, including Dr.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese airlines protest US plan to ban their flights over Russian airspace
item.description: The U.S. side has said such flights give Chinese airlines an unfair cost advantage over American carriers, which cannot cross through Russian airspace.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Snipers on stadium roof amid heavy security for Italy's win over Israel in World Cup qualifying
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The World Cup qualifier between Italy and Israel took place amid a heavy police presence that included snipers on the stadium roof.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners freed as Trump tours region
item.description: Hamas released all 20 of the last living hostages on Monday, the Israeli military said, as part of a breakthrough ceasefire after two years of war between Israel and Hamas in the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy to visit Washington this week seeking long-range weapons and a Trump meeting
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday he will travel to the United States this week for talks on the potential U.S. provision of long-range weapons, a day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners are released as part of ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — All 20 remaining living hostages held in Gaza and hundreds of Palestinian prisoners held by Israel walked free Monday as part of a ceasefire pausing two years of war that decimated the Gaza Strip and killed tens of thousands of Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: From a hilltop village and across Israel: Tears of joy as the living hostages are returned
item.description: LAVON, Israel (AP) — It was a day of joy and tears across Israel. Families, loved ones and friends watched as Hamas released the last living hostages held captive for over two years in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel expects hostages to be freed from Gaza 'in a few hours' as ceasefire holds
item.description: Israel said Sunday that it expected all living hostages held in Gaza to be released Monday in its breakthrough ceasefire deal with Hamas, as Palestinians awaited the release of hundreds of prisoners held in Israel and a surge of aid into the famine-stricken territory.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, SARAH EL DEEB and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
